steps.title: Fastest path: mark the figure first
steps.content: For coordinate-circle questions, read the center and radius from the equation or graph the circle as a check. For diagram facts, mark equal radii, diameters, tangents, and arcs before doing algebra.

steps.title: Read the center and radius
steps.content: The equation $(x+4)^{2}+(y-15)^{2}=144$ is in standard circle form $(x-h)^{2}+(y-k)^{2}=r^{2}$, giving center $(-4,15)$ and radius $r=\sqrt{144}=12$.

steps.title: Bound the x-coordinate of any point on the circle
steps.content: For a point $(a,b)$ on the circle, $a$ must lie within one radius of the center's $x$-coordinate: $-4-12 \le a \le -4+12$, i.e. $-16 \le a \le 8$.

steps.title: Test each choice against the interval
steps.content:
Compare each value of $a$ to $[-16,8]$:
  • A) $a=-17$: $-17\lt -16$, outside the interval — eliminate.
  • B) $a=-10$: $-16 \le -10 \le 8$, inside the interval — possible.
  • C) $a=12$: $12\gt 8$, outside the interval — eliminate.
  • D) $a=15$: $15\gt 8$, outside the interval — eliminate.
That confirms B is correct.
